title: Pulmonary Anatomy & Physiology
description: Structure and functional dynamics of the respiratory system, gas exchange, regulation of breathing, and acid-base regulation.

lessons.title: Structure and Functional Dynamics of the Respiratory System
lessons.description: Upper and lower respiratory anatomy, conducting vs respiratory zones, epithelial types and PCCE, thoracic cavity and lungs, respiratory zone and pulmonary blood flow, pulmonary ventilation, lung volumes and capacities.

lessons.title: Gas Exchange
lessons.description: Energy, temperature and gas pressure, volume and weight laws, ideal gas laws, gas transportation by blood, gas diffusion laws, and the mechanics of external and internal gas exchange.

lessons.title: Regulation of Breathing
lessons.description: Medullary rhythmicity center, pneumotaxic and apneustic centers of the pons, central and peripheral chemoreceptors, and proprioceptor stimulation.

lessons.title: Acid Base Regulation
lessons.description: Hydrolysis equation, pH regulation, CO2 and HCO3- balance, protein buffer systems, and the carbonic acid-bicarbonate buffer system.

lessons.title: Module Exam
lessons.description: Comprehensive 50-question exam covering all Pulmonary Anatomy & Physiology content.

resources.title: Mucociliary Escalator
resources.description: Visual demonstration of mucociliary clearance mechanism.

resources.title: Respiratory System Overview
resources.description: Comprehensive overview of the respiratory system.

resources.title: Respiratory System Anatomy
resources.description: Detailed anatomy of the respiratory system.

resources.title: Pulmonary Blood Flow
resources.description: Blood flow through the pulmonary circulation.

resources.title: LaPlace's Law and Lung Recoil
resources.description: LaPlace's law applied to lung mechanics.

resources.title: Partial Pressures
resources.description: Understanding partial pressures in respiratory physiology.

resources.title: Gas Exchange
resources.description: How gas exchange occurs in the lungs.
